title: Board of Directors - 211info
text: Erick Maddox, Chair Erick Maddox is Executive Director of Reliance eHealth Collaborative, a Medford-based Regional Health Information Exchange. In this role he works closely with the Office of Health Information Technology at the Oregon Health Authority and with a range of local, regional, and statewide health systems. Erick has expertise in health care operations, health information technology, data integration, and data analysis. He supports 211info’s focus on the social determinants of health in the social services and health care sectors. Get to Know 211info: Meet Erick Ned Mossman, Vice Chair Ned Mossman oversees the Value-Based Care and Social Determinants of Health programs at OCHIN, a Portland-based health information network made up of over 100 Community Health Centers nationwide. Before joining OCHIN in 2014, Ned served for seven years as Associate Director of the Oregon Osteoporosis Center. Graham Bouldin, Secretary Graham Bouldin is Chief Quality Officer for Health Share of Oregon, where he oversees the organization’s Quality Improvement, Analytics and Information Technology. He has a background in youth mental health and Medicaid managed care. Bouldin previously served as a mental health intensive services care coordinator and quality assurance coordinator for Clackamas County Behavioral Health. Angela Mitchell, Treasurer Angela Mitchell is Vice President of Value Based Purchasing for CareOregon . Previously she served as U.S. Healthcare Delivery Systems Manager for Intel; in Patient & Family Centered Care and Hospice for Providence; and as Relief Nursery Program Manager for Volunteer and Support Services. She has an executive master’s in public administration with a focus on healthcare reform from Portland State University. Piseth Pich, Immediate Past Chair Piseth Pich joined the board in 2016 and has interests in emergency management, health care, and public affairs. Pich is public information officer for Hillsboro Fire and Rescue, and previously worked for Legacy Health. Pich served as public affairs officer, volunteer program support coordinator, and firefighter for Tualatin Valley Fire & Rescue. He is also an active member and volunteer for the Portland chapter of the Public Relations Society of America. Get to Know 211info: Meet Piseth Craig Opperman, Member Craig Opperman has served as president/CEO of Looking Glass Community Services in Eugene, Oregon, since 2004. Looking Glass helps “build a better future” for more than 9,000 children, youth, adults and families who are working through abuse, neglect, educational challenges, mental health, and alcohol/drug issues and homelessness. He brings to 211info a strong knowledge of organizational operations, social service systems, and policy/program development. Michael Pope, Member Michael Pope is a healthcare-focused consultant who is Program Manager for the Oregon Health Leadership Council/HIT Commons. He has a master’s of public administration/health policy from George Mason University. Martin Taylor, Member Martin Taylor, an active member of the 211info board since 2010, has wide-ranging Oregon legislative, political, and healthcare experience. He has served as executive director of the Oregon Nurses Association and as executive director of Public Affairs and Marketing at CareOregon. Taylor's extensive experience in health care and legislative policy assists 211info's strategies in those arenas. Tracy Carver, Member Tracy Carver is Comagine Health’s Sr. Director, Community Health. She leads the Community Health Service Line and has over 15 years of experience leading large-scale, complex federal health initiatives. She is a trusted thought leader in the areas of chronic disease prevention and management and community care coordination models. Tracy also serves as co-chair for the Civitas Networks For Health Social Determinants of Health (SDOH) and Interoperability Workgroup. She holds a Master of Public Administration from Portland State University. Celia Higueras, Member Raised in Chile, Celia Higueras is a bicultural public health leader who received her Bachelor of Arts from Lewis and Clark College and her Master of Public Health from Portland State University. She has worked to improve community health and access to healthcare and social services in Oregon, New York, and Chile. Celia created the first Latina Services Program at the Sexual Assault Resource Center to support Latina women and families. She helped grow the Oregon Community Health Workers Association to advocate for Community Health Workers and led the implementation of a statewide Community Information Exchange with over 1,000 participating organizations to enhance care coordination. She currently serves as the Health Systems Manager at the Oregon Health Authority Office of Community Engagement and Health. Celia loves reading and going on adventures with her family. Her superpower is bringing diverse groups together to rally for the same cause and never forgetting to bring snacks. Anne King, Member coming soon Learn More 211info is always looking to engage volunteer professionals, whose expertise can help guide the organization’s work. If you are interested in serving on our Board of Directors, please complete the form below.
